text:
Report × Darkness × Released Chain (時報×暗闇×放たれた鎖, Jihō × Kurayami × Hanatareta Kusari) is the 67 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and is the 5th episode of the Yorknew City OVA series. It was released on DVD on March 20 th , 2002.

Overview

Gon and Killua attempt to escape unsuccessfully. The Phantom Troupe boss, Chrollo Lucilfer, is captured by Kurapika.

Synopsis

Three minutes before seven o'clock, Pakunoda, Nobunaga, and Kortopi arrive at the Hotel Beitacle to meet with Chrollo's group. Nobunaga is excited to see Gon and Killua, assuming that they are there with the Troupe because they have decided to join them, but Killua insists that they were only after the bounty on the Spiders' heads and didn't know the bounty had been called off already. Nobunaga continues to joke around with the boys about them getting captured again, and Gon and Killua are immediately able to notice that he smells of blood, although they are unsure who he had just killed. Despite the boys' continued resistance to join the Troupe, Nobunaga continues to talk them up to Chrollo, who then asks Pakunoda to check the boys again for memories of the Chain User.

The boys start to panic, as they won't be able to hide their memories of Kurapika anymore now that they know he's the Chain User, but it is still too early to put their plan into action. Killua and Gon attempt to talk to Pakunoda to stall her and discourage her from checking their memories again, but she starts choking the boys in response, making it difficult for them to speak. However, the clock then strikes seven o'clock, and the lobby goes completely dark. In the dark, Killua is quickly able to get himself out of Machi's bindings. He and Gon are able to strike Pakunoda and Machi, but Gon, still in Machi's bindings, gets pulled back towards her. Killua tries to help him, but Machi also manages to grab him. Nobunaga uses En to sense the boys, and can grab ahold of Gon before Gon can do anything further.

A knife flies by Nobunaga's head, just missing him, and sticks in the wall behind him. He looks towards the entrance where it came from, but can't see anyone there, even as his eyes start to adjust to the dark. When lightning flashes, the Troupe is able to get a brief look around and notices that Chrollo is missing. shizuku holds onto Gon while Nobunaga takes the knife out of the wall and finds a note attached to it. Nobunaga flicks on his lighter to read it before handing it to Pakunoda. She reads the not, and is then able to use her ability on the note to see what transpired in the dark, and is shown Kurapika, who had been disguised as a receptionist, capturing Chrollo with his chain. Pakunoda is amazed and perplexed by how the boys, along with their allies, came up with and executed their plan to capture Chrollo in such a short amount of time. Pakunoda is hopeful, given that Gon and Killua seem to be considered hostages in this situation, that they will still be able to save Chrollo somehow.

The Troupe, unable to leave Gon and Killua alone, decides to wait until Phink's group arrives before making any moves. Pakunoda recalls when Chrollo first outlined the hierarchy and rules of the Phantom Troupe to its members. She then questions what to do and if she should tell the other Troupe members what she saw, since Chrollo had been adamant about not prioritizing his life over the whole Spider's. Pakunoda gets caught up thinking that this moment of decision is what was outlined in her prophecy, and she starts overthinking it. She tries to talk herself down since her prophecy foretold the events of next week, not this day, but she is still very worried about Chrollo's life and what decision he would make in her shoes. Machi's voice startles Pakunoda out of her thoughts, and Machi cryptically advises her to stay quiet about whatever she was thinking.

The lights in the lobby get restored, and Nobunaga calls Phinks on the phone. Pakunoda, not wanting to lose Chrollo, decides she is willing to go against the rules of the Spider if it means getting him back.
